relation_to_my_preds:
### Prediction 1: Malicious local LLMs will begin to function like malware.

Today's report has no direct case of "a local LLM itself going malware," but peripheral signals thicken further:

- **SGLang CVE-2026-5760** (Jinja2 SSTI through chat_template / GGUF, CVSS 9.8 RCE) and **Pipecat CVE-2025-62373** (CVSS 9.8 RCE via pickle deserialization, a Python voice-multimodal conversation-agent framework) — two RCE paths surface simultaneously through which a local AI agent runtime gets RCE'd by a malicious model / payload. References: [SGLang 2026 Q2 Roadmap](https://github.com/sgl-project/sglang/issues/22949) / [vuln.today](https://vuln.today/)
- **10 in-the-wild Indirect Prompt Injection** payloads cross web-content poisoning / RAG / HTML-comment processing — local LLMs trigger malicious behavior through ingested context. References: [Infosecurity Magazine](https://www.infosecurity-magazine.com/news/researchers-10-wild-indirect/)
- Important addition — **Exabeam establishes an "AI Insider Threat" category**, building behavior-detection across ChatGPT / Copilot / Gemini under the framing "AI agents act as insiders with credentials / privileges." A market-side response layer (Agent Behavior Analytics) is consolidating against the user prediction's "local LLMs functioning like malware." References: [SiliconANGLE - Exabeam expands ABA across ChatGPT, Copilot, Gemini](https://siliconangle.com/2026/04/01/exabeam-expands-agent-behavior-analytics-secure-ai-agents-across-chatgpt-copilot-gemini/)

### Prediction 2 (today's framing): RL/LLM-based forecasting performance improvement spreads.

Today: **no direct case**. Adjacent signals: Anthropic's quality post-mortem + GPT-5.5's planning / tool / self-check + Galileo Luna-2 evaluators (auto-identifies agent drift).

### Prediction 3 (today's framing): Power / compute scarcity drives AI SaaS price increases.

**Strongest correlation** today — GPT-5.5 API at 2x, Alphabet 2026 capex $175-185B (2x of 2025), Tesla capex >$25B, ServiceNow and other Legacy SaaS chained-fall, plus a new SaaS billing line emerging at the agent-security layer (Keycard's $38M / Okta Showcase / Zenity Fortune Cyber 60) — a two-pole structure forms.
